summary: Hotz argues that technology has been quietly captured by power structures in ways most people don't even remember or recognize. Drawing on the dystopian novel 'Manna,' he poses the fundamental question of whether machines will serve humanity or enslave it. He contends that centralized technology inevitably leads to a zero-sum power struggle that no one truly wins—even the final 'winner' ends up with worthless dominance. The post is a direct plea to technologists to stop building centralized systems and instead focus on decentralized alternatives before it's too late.
key_points: Tech culture has been infiltrated and transformed so gradually that people don't remember it was ever different
Centralized technology leads inevitably to slavery and endless power struggles with no real winners
The concentration of all power/money in one person makes that power meaningless
Technologists bear personal responsibility for the systems they build and where those systems lead society
The timeline for these consequences is not distant future but roughly 20 years away
money_quotes: Tech got inflitrated in such a way that you forgot that it was ever different.
Will the machines work for us or will we work for the machines?
People will not give up their desire for power over other people.
If technology is centralized, you will be a slave.
You will not be one of the 6 winners.
If one person ends up with all the money, the money is worthless.
Your vacations aren't worth it. Your house isn't worth it. Eating fancy food isn't worth it.
If everyone acted like you, where is the world going?
key_insight: Centralized technology is a trap that leads to universal enslavement, and technologists must personally reckon with their role in building it before the 20-year countdown runs out.
